response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates excellent software engineering fundamentals and adaptability across diverse technologies, they fall far short of the mandatory requirements for this role. With only ~1 year of documented C++ experience (versus 5–10 required), no Windows platform development background, no fixed income or capital markets domain expertise, and limited enterprise SQL Server/Oracle experience, the ramp-up required would be impractical for a mid-senior level consulting engagement in a specialized capital markets domain.
strengths: Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, and testing—transferable discipline for capital markets systems
Proven ability to learn and contribute across diverse domains (web, mobile, embedded, GIS, fintech) with multiple language/framework transitions
Clear communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and well-articulated technical documentation
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ depth: only ~1 year of documented experience (2014–2015 at National Instruments) versus 5–10 years mandatory
Complete absence of fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; most recent fintech exposure (RIDE Capital) used Node/TypeScript, not C++
No Windows platform development background and minimal enterprise SQL Server/Oracle experience; career focused on web, MongoDB, and non-Windows systems
